title: Cirrus: Notify on some post-merge failures
body:
There is currently no simple way in Cirrus to perform actions when a
build or task has failed.  However, this means it's easily possible for
a flaking test or conflict to become part of master.  Add a
wrapper-script to monitor for some of these conditions, and post a
notice on IRC if/when it happens.

***Note:*** This is temporary.  An upstream RFE, marked "Comming Soon" will provide a
complete solution when it's released.  https://github.com/cirruslabs/cirrus-ci-docs/issues/55

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* To fully approve this pull request, please assign additional approvers. We suggest the following additional approver: **tomsweeneyredhat** If they are not already assigned, you can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @tomsweeneyredhat` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

***NOTE:*** This is not a perfect solution - it will ONLY capture failures in commands which are wrapped.  It also requires adding the wrapper script to every single instruction which needs monitoring.

Shortcomings:
* It won't catch any `gate` or build_each_commit` task failures.
* It requires someone to be on IRC to notice it happen.
* It reports on EVERY VM that fails a build (could get noisy).

It's certainly better than what we have right now, where absolutely no notification that we're repeatedly failing CI happens.

Question: can we possibly include terminal-escape codes in the IRC message? I would really love a bold red error for this so we can't easily ignore it

@mheon @baude PTAL, this is not a perfect solution - it won't catch all failures, and it will be quite noisy.  However until the Cirrus-CI "on failure" feature (linked above) works, it's the best we got.  I instrumented the code to fail in this PR, and got this in IRC:

```
[10:36] <podbot_65211700> Action Required: Post-merge testing on fedora 28, unit_test.sh failed (exit 2): https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5728136699314176
[10:38] <podbot_46915826> Action Required: Post-merge testing on ubuntu 18, unit_test.sh failed (exit 2): https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5728136699314176
```

Without my instrumenting the code, it should only complain about failures after merging on master (not from PRs).

>  I would really love a bold red error for this so we can't easily ignore it

IRC doesn't use regular escape codes, and different IRC clients may handle color codes differently.  However, there is a general standard for most clients, I added in the code for red, but it may not work for everybody.
LGTM on my end. Multiple notifications isn't optimal but it's better than 0 notifications.

> Multiple notifications isn't optimal but it's better than 0 notifications.

Ya, best we can have for now.  Multiple people have requested 'on failure' task capability in Cirrus.  When that happens, this can easily be cut down to one notice per failed build (instead of failed task).

Lolz...[the upstream feature _just_ merged](https://github.com/cirruslabs/cirrus-ci-docs/issues/55) - allows running specified scripts on job failure.  @mheon @baude What's your opinion, go forward with this (messy/imperfect) workaround, or I'll close this and [work up an implementation using the new feature](https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/writing-tasks/#execution-behavior-of-instructions)?

(side-benefit here is, we can now capture log files / debugging details even on job failure)
@cevich If it's not too much work I'd be in favor of the new on-fail trigger, but it's really up to you, since you're doing the work

@mheon are you suggesting I should do the work? :grin:   Let me be more clear: Implementing the new feature is (guessing) a day/three work (at most).  Can y'all afford that extra time for this feature?

I'm guessing "yes", and I'm happy to do the work, just wanted to confirm a delay is okay :smile:
@cevich Take all the time you need - the postmerge tests have mostly settled down, though we seem to have a lot more flakes popping up recently
